subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus.
Research question is clearly stated and guides the investigation.
Logical organization with detailed table of contents and consistent signposting aids reader comprehension.
weaknesses: Wording of the research question (âto what extentâ) suggests an evaluative angle not fully realized in the discussion.
Methodology lacks sufficient detail on control of external variables and calibration procedures.
Background section includes historical digressions that are tangential to the core experimental focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates the investigationâs focus with precision (band 5â6), and the research question is explicit and well aligned with the experimental design (band 5â6). The methodology is generally complete with relevant methods and evidence of informed source selection, but it omits some controls and calibration details (band 3â4). Overall, performance across the three strands justifies a mark of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates secure knowledge of electromagnetism and particle physics through correct derivations and application of the e/m formula.
Terminology (e.g., Helmholtz coils, centripetal force, error propagation) is used accurately and consistently.
Graphs and uncertainty analyses reflect coherent understanding of core physical concepts.
weaknesses: Occasional numerical slips (e.g., inconsistent râ»Â² values) dilute scientific precision.
Some phrasing is colloquial or vague (e.g., âmodification in spaceâ), reducing formality.
Historical exposition sometimes overextends beyond necessary application to current experiment.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows good subject knowledge and mostly accurate use of terminology, placing it firmly in the 3â4 band. Minor numerical and stylistic errors prevent a topâband mark, so a 4 out of 6 is appropriate.

strengths: Clear, logical progression from theoretical derivation through data analysis to conclusion.
Wellâstructured argument that integrates tables and graphs to support claims.
Quantitative data analysis includes uncertainty propagation and multiple statistical measures.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation of error sources remains at a surface level with limited quantitative assessment of impact.
Discussion periodically digresses into historical narrative rather than deepening analysis.
Proposed methodological improvements are general and lack specificity on how they would enhance precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Across the criticalâthinking strands, the student delivers good research analysis and a coherent argument (meeting 7â9 band descriptors) and offers a satisfactory level of critical evaluation. Performance aligns with the lower end of the 7â9 band, warranting 7 out of 12.

strengths: Follows IB structural conventions with title page, abstract, numbered sections, and references.
Figures and tables are generally well labelled and integrated into the text.
Use of headings and page numbering supports navigation.
weaknesses: Equation and table formatting is occasionally inconsistent, detracting from polish.
Quality of some visual elements is variable (lowâresolution images, misaligned tables).
Table of contents is overly detailed, making navigation cumbersome.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report meets the descriptors for clear and appropriate structure and effective layout (3â4 band), but uneven formatting and visual inconsistencies limit it to a midâband score of 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Physics terminologyâHelmholtz coils, centripetal force, permeability, propagation of error, etc.âis used frequently and largely correctly. Equations are presented with symbols defined, and units are specified.  

There are minor inconsistencies (e.g., misuse of comma in vector equation notation, some unit slips in tables) and occasional awkward phrasing, but these do not impede understanding. The breadth and mostly accurate deployment of concepts satisfy the descriptors for 3â4; the higher mark of 4 reflects generally sound, though not flawless, command of subject language.

The argument progresses logically from theoretical derivation through experimental design, data presentation, analysis, and conclusion. Tables and graphs are integrated to support claims, and the final e/m value is compared with the accepted constant. The student discusses proportional relationships and addresses discrepancies through percentage error analysis.  

Yet, the discussion sometimes digresses into historical narrative without strengthening the central argument, and the justification of conclusions in light of identified errors is limited. While coherent and generally well-structured, the argument lacks the sophistication and critical linkage expected for the top bands, positioning it comfortably in the 4â6 range with a score of 5.

The methodology includes a comprehensive list of apparatus, a step-by-step procedure, definition of variables, detailed derivations for key equations and extensive data-handling routines (error propagation, graphing, statistical treatment). Sources for theoretical constants and formulae are referenced, showing some informed selection.  

However, replication, control of external variables (e.g., Earthâs field, temperature), and justification of chosen voltage and radius ranges are discussed only briefly. Calibration procedures are mentioned but not demonstrated with evidence. These gaps indicate the methodology is mostly complete but not consistently rigorous, placing it solidly in the 3â4 band; the top value of 4 is merited for the generally competent but not exemplary design.

The student demonstrates secure knowledge of electromagnetism and particle physics: derivation of the e/m formula from Lorentz and centripetal forces, explanation of Helmholtz coil field equation, and historical context of Thomsonâs work. Graphs, calculations and discussion of uncertainties reflect correct application of physics principles.  

Occasional numerical slips (e.g., erroneous râ»Â² values in Table 1) and some over-extended historical exposition dilute the scientific focus. Nonetheless, the core concepts are applied accurately and mostly coherently to interpret results, meeting the criteria for the 3â4 band at its upper end (score 4).

The evaluation section identifies both systematic (instrument calibration, field uniformity) and random (parallax, eye strain, Earthâs field) errors and proposes some mitigations. The student also interprets different uncertainty metrics and comments on their suitability.  

Nonetheless, critical reflection is somewhat surface-level: proposed improvements remain general, and there is limited exploration of how specific uncertainties quantitatively influence the final e/m value or how alternative methods might yield more reliable results. This constitutes a good but not consistently critical evaluation, aligning with the 4â6 band; a score of 4 reflects the lower end of that band due to the noted limitations.

The student states the topicâmeasuring the electron-specific charge with a Helmholtz coilâprominently in the title, abstract section and throughout the report. The background narrative and justification are well developed, linking the constant (e/m) to wider scientific relevance (e.g., spectroscopy, cosmology) and citing authoritative sources such as Hawking and Thomson.  

Organisation is logical: a detailed table of contents, numbered sections, clear subsection headings and consistent signposting ensure the reader can follow the investigationâs purpose without ambiguity.  

Minor lapses in concision (occasional digressions into historical context) do not significantly detract from the clarity of focus. Overall, communication of the topic is accurate, purposeful and effective, fully matching the 5â6 band; the higher mark of 6 is warranted for its sustained precision and reader guidance.

Photographs of apparatus, multiple annotated graphs and neatly formatted tables aid comprehension and are generally well integrated with captions and numbering. Page numbers, headings and use of bold/italics enhance navigation.  

Some visual elements (e.g., scanned textbook diagrams, low-resolution images) are of variable quality, and colour coding in graphs is not always explained in text. Occasional misalignment of tables and inconsistent significant-figure presentation slightly detract from professionalism. Nonetheless, layout supports clear communication overall, satisfying the top presentation band; a score of 3 (mid-band) is justified given the minor shortcomings.

The research questionââTo what extent can the (e/m) ratio of an electron be precisely determined by considering both the radius of its circular path and the influence of external magnetic fields?ââis explicitly stated and clearly aligned with the investigation. It focuses on two measurable parameters (r and B) and directs the subsequent discussion and data analysis.  

Nevertheless, the wording âto what extentâ suggests an evaluative angle that is only partially explored; the essay largely concentrates on obtaining a numerical value rather than explicitly delineating criteria for assessing âprecision.â This slight mismatch in intent versus execution justifies awarding the lower mark (5) within the 5â6 band.

Data sets cover seven voltages and four radii, producing multiple graphs. The student applies linearisation (1/rÂ² vs BÂ²) and determines gradients to extract e/m, demonstrating a clear analytical pathway tied to the research question. Uncertainty propagation and several statistical measures (range, mean deviation, standard deviation) show awareness of data quality.  

However, some calculated values and unit labels reveal errors (e.g., râ»Â² table inconsistency, omission of significant calibration discussion). Treatment of background magnetic fields remains qualitative. These shortcomings limit the depth and reliability of evaluation, but overall analysis is still coherent and relevant, fulfilling the 4â6 band; a mid-high score of 5 is appropriate.

The essay follows IB EE structural conventions: title page with word count, abstract/intro, sequentially numbered sections, tables, figures, references and an appendix. Citations are present and most figures/tables are labelled. Minor inconsistencies (e.g., over-detailed table of contents, occasional formatting glitches in equations) do not obscure readability.  

Because the overall framework is clear and complete, but polish is uneven (long tables split awkwardly, some equation formatting errors), the work meets the descriptor for the 3â4 band; a score of 3 reflects sound but not flawless execution.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is overly detailed, with deep sub-levels that may overwhelm the reader; streamline headings to improve navigability and focus on primary sections.
Figure 7 is well chosen but lacks an explicit mention of the reference plane in the caption; specify the field direction relative to the page plane for full clarity.

The title is clear and focused, explicitly stating the investigation of the e/m ratio using a Helmholtz coil, which provides strong guidance for the reader and aligns well with the defined research scope.

The research question phrasing âto what extentâ suggests an evaluative focus that is not fully realized later; consider revising to explicitly match investigation aims or ensuring evaluation criteria are addressed in the discussion.
The word count is precisely indicated as 4000 words, demonstrating adherence to IB formatting requirements.
The introduction uses colloquial phrasing (âI was fond to discoverâ) and anecdotal tone, which is less appropriate for a formal scientific report; adopt a more objective voice.
Figure 1 caption lacks scale or dimensional annotations for Sâ and Sâ slits, which impedes the readerâs ability to fully interpret the apparatus setup.
Figures 3 and 4 use clear, consistent styling and alt text to convey magnetic field patterns, effectively supporting visual learning.
The research question is clearly and prominently stated in its own section, directly guiding the subsequent methodology and analysis.
The aim succinctly defines the experimental goal and references the theoretical e/m value, demonstrating accurate application of subject knowledge.
Citation of Thomson (2024) appears inconsistent with original publication dates, indicating potential referencing errors; verify source details to ensure accuracy.
There is no mention of planned replication or control trials in the procedure; including these would strengthen the investigationâs reliability and overall methodological rigor.

The extensive historical background on atomic theory is interesting but tangential to the core investigation; focus background more tightly on concepts directly relevant to the Helmholtz coil experiment.
The relationship W=Â½ mvÂ² is correctly applied to link potential energy change to kinetic energy, showing strong understanding of energy principles.
The methodology section devotes excessive detail to historical experiments rather than clearly describing the studentâs own experimental design, limiting methodological clarity.

The description of the magnetic field uses the term âmodification in spaceâ which is vague; replace with precise definitions (e.g., âmagnetic field is a vector field representing force per unit poleâ).
Terminology such as âAdvanced atomic theoryâ and citation of primary references demonstrates appropriate use of subject concepts and supports understanding.
The CRT diagram is highâquality and well-annotated, greatly aiding comprehension of the historical experiment and its relevance to the present work.
The resolution of Figure 2 is low, leading to pixelation; consider replacing with a higherâresolution or vector graphic to maintain clarity when enlarged.
Repetitive âNote. From Homerâ¦â captions for multiple figures could be consolidated or moved to a combined source list to reduce redundancy in the text.
Units for Âµâ are described inconsistently (âTÂ·m/Aâ vs âTÂ·m/Aâ); ensure uniform unit notation throughout tables and lists for professionalism.
The explanation that field lines are âmostly condensed at the middleâ lacks quantitative detail; include field uniformity measurements or simulations for stronger evidence.
Equation 1 is correctly formatted in display mode, with all variables defined below, enhancing the clarity of the theoretical framework.
The Lorentz force equation is missing the cross-product symbol; replace âqvBâsinâ¦â with âq vÃBâ for precise vector notation.
The derivation of centripetal force leading to the final squared velocity expression is methodical and demonstrates strong analytical reasoning.

The introductory sentence to the Helmholtz coil section lacks explicit reference to how the studentâs apparatus differs from textbook setups; include such details to contextualize your experimental design.
Controlled variables are listed with uncertainties but lack rationale for their selection; justify why these tolerances were chosen in relation to expected measurement precision.
